steps.title: Test the color hypothesis
steps.content: The team's hypothesis requires yellow-tinted dissolved organic carbon to explain why some blue ice appears green. If that cause is correct, green icebergs should differ from blue icebergs in the amount or effect of that carbon.

steps.title: Reject non-weakening findings
steps.content:
• Choice A concerns white ice, not the blue-to-green explanation.
  • Choice B supports the idea that Antarctic carbon could add yellow color.
  • Choice C says the two colors are rarely near each other, but it does not test their composition.
The remaining choice says blue and green icebergs contain similarly small traces of the proposed coloring agent. The answer is D.
